app.name: E-App
app.tagline: Home energy analysis – easy to do yourself.

info.buildingYear: The year of construction reveals the typical insulation standard and helps estimate the energy demand.
info.windowAge: Older windows often have higher heat losses – the age helps with the assessment.
info.ventilation: HRV stands for heat recovery: the ventilation uses the warmth of the exhaust air to pre-heat fresh air.
info.insulation: A rough self-assessment is enough – you don't need to measure anything precisely.
info.goals: Your goals help the app prioritise the right measurements and tips.
info.occupancy: As an owner, larger measures are often possible than when renting.
info.persons: The number of people significantly affects hot water and electricity use.
info.livingArea: The heated living area is a key figure for the heating demand.
info.heatGenerators: Multiple selection is possible if several systems heat in parallel.
info.hotWater: Is the hot water produced by the heating system or by a separate device?
info.pv: A PV system supplies your own electricity and changes the energy balance.
info.fireplace: An additional fireplace/stove adds room heat and should be recorded.
info.instruments: Select the devices you own – per device you can pick a type and see recommendations.
info.smartHome: Smart devices can provide consumption data and improve the analysis.
info.energyCost: A rough estimate is enough to express savings potential in euros.
info.renovation: Renovations improve the insulation standard and reduce the energy demand.
info.location: Your postal code lets regional climate and weather data flow in later.

temp_infrared_pro.name: Infrared Thermometer Pro
temp_infrared_pro.benefit: Measure surface temperatures contactlessly in seconds.

temp_room_smart.name: Smart Room Thermometer
temp_room_smart.benefit: Keep room temperature in view, with app connectivity.

distance_laser.name: Laser Distance Meter
distance_laser.benefit: Measure rooms and areas quickly and precisely.

co2_monitor.name: CO₂ Monitor
co2_monitor.benefit: Monitor air quality and ventilate in good time.

power_plug.name: Energy Cost Meter
power_plug.benefit: Easily track down power hogs in your home.

tariff.title: Electricity price

prompt.title: Do you know your electricity price?
prompt.text: With your electricity price the app can calculate real euro amounts instead of just kWh.

tariff.workPriceInfo: Price per kilowatt-hour of electricity consumed.
tariff.basePriceInfo: Fixed monthly base fee independent of consumption.
tariff.cardInfo: The electricity price is used to convert your consumption into real euro amounts.

whereToFind.title: Where do I find my electricity price?
whereToFind.text: On your last electricity bill or in your supply contract. The unit price is given in ct/kWh, the base price in €/month.

tariff.skipHint: We'll then calculate with average values.
soonPoints: Enter meter readings for electricity, water, gas and pellets
Consumption shown as a clear chart

onboarding.title: Setup
onboarding.subtitle: A few questions about your home – so the app can guide you properly.
points: Rooms, living area and year the building was built
Heating type (heat pump, gas, oil, pellets, wood …)
Hot water: central, decentral or via the heating system
Number of residents
Available measuring devices (thermometer, power meter …)

measurements.title: Measurements
measurements.subtitle: The core: simple, guided measurements step by step.
points: Guided flow: explanation → measurement → result
Examples like the shower head test or fridge check
Instantly understandable tips for each result
Sort by room, value or recommended order

monitoring.title: Monitoring
monitoring.subtitle: Record meter readings and track consumption over time.
points: Enter meter readings for electricity, water, gas, pellets
Consumption shown as a clear chart
Reminders for regular readings
Spot trends and anomalies

Evaluation of the energy monitoring
Choose a short or long version
Export as PDF to share and archive

education.title: Learn
education.subtitle: Background, FAQ and learning content about home energy.
points: FAQ and further information about the measurements
Clear explanations for everyone
Section for HTW Berlin: Building Energy and Information Technology
Lab experiments with preparation and a short quiz

hero.title: Estimated energy costs
hero.label: Estimated energy costs / year
hero.info: This is a rough estimate based on your profile (people, living area) and the stored electricity price. With real measurements and meter readings the figure becomes much more accurate.

tips: Lower the room temperature by 1 °C – it saves about 6 % of heating energy.
Standby devices quietly draw power: switchable power strips help.
Air the room briefly with wide-open windows instead of tilting them to save heat and prevent mould.
When buying new appliances, choose the highest energy efficiency class.
Washing laundry at 30 °C instead of 60 °C saves up to two thirds of the electricity.
Set the fridge to 7 °C – any colder just wastes energy.
